based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/xor-per-neuron-bias.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted v0.4 canonical bytes, per-neuron biases on h1/h2/y with bias_policy.mode=sgd). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

mutation.delta_vs_tolerance_multiplier: ~32300x (effective tolerance ~3.09e-11 at this magnitude under {atol: 1e-12, rtol: 1e-8}; delta 1e-6 is ~32310x)
what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[6].gradient (0.00618790217 — Rule 4 still passes: product(factors) still equals stored gradient)
updates[6].weight_before (0.100000000 — left operand of Rule 6 sum)
updates[6].weight_after (0.103093951 — derived from the ORIGINAL update; Rule 6 will cascade since stored weight_after no longer equals weight_before + mutated update)
parameters_after.b_h1 (0.103093951 — Rule 7 cascades for the same reason)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 5 failure naming updates[6].update on parameter b_h1. Rule 4 must NOT fire (gradient is unchanged). Rules 6 and 7 cascade from Rule 5 — both observe parameter_id 'b_h1' with cascade_of_rule = 5.
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 6 on updates[6].weight_after (b_h1): stored weight_after (0.103093951) was derived from original update; recomputed = weight_before + mutated update differs by ~1e-6 (cascade_of_rule: 5)

Rule 7 on parameters_after.b_h1: stored parameters_after.b_h1 (0.103093951) was derived from original update; recomputed = parameters_before.b_h1 + mutated update differs by ~1e-6 (cascade_of_rule: 5)
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures. The fixture_status block being deliberately_corrupted / expected_to_fail_reconciliation must NOT mask the Rule 5 failure.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage) extended into the v0.4 per-neuron bias corner: proves the reconciler checks Rule 5 (update == lr * gradient) on bias updates by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the rtol-scaled tolerance) between stored update and the product of (unmutated) lr * gradient. Cascade into Rules 6, 7 demonstrates the cascade_of_rule wiring works for bias updates too.
v0_4_note: Per-neuron bias updates participate in Rule 5/6/7 the same way weight updates do — the bias parameter appears in parameter_order and updates[] alongside weight parameters. Cascade detection wires the same way: a Rule 5 failure on a bias update will cascade to Rule 6 and Rule 7 if those rules read the same stored update value.
